cases.name: sync-then-detach-clean-one-round
cases.description: A clean syncThenDetach uploads the only dirty row, detaches in one round, clears managed state, and rotates the source.

cases.name: sync-then-detach-late-write-second-round
cases.description: A local write that arrives during the first pull blocks the first detach attempt, then drains in a second sync round.

cases.name: sync-then-detach-no-progress-blocks
cases.description: Fresh writes that keep arriving without reducing pending count stop syncThenDetach early and preserve attached dirty state.

cases.name: rebuild-required-restart-keep-source
cases.description: A generic rebuild-required gate survives restart and normal sync automatically resumes keep-source snapshot recovery; explicit rebuild remains available.

sql: UPDATE _sync_operation_state SET kind = 'none', reason = 'checkpoint_ahead' WHERE singleton_key = 1

cases.name: remote-authoritative-partial-replace-restart-restages
cases.description: A failed remote-authoritative attach keeps remote_replace staged; restart restages a fresh snapshot and applies only fresh remote state.

cases.name: pending-remote-replace-detach-cancels-stage
cases.description: Detaching during a pending remote_replace clears the staged snapshot and returns to anonymous state on the same source.

sql: INSERT INTO _sync_snapshot_stage(snapshot_id, row_ordinal, schema_name, table_name, key_json, row_version, payload) VALUES ('snapshot-1', 0, 'main', 'users', '{"id":"user-1"}', 1, '{"id":"user-1","name":"Ada"}')
UPDATE _sync_operation_state SET kind = 'remote_replace', target_user_id = 'user-1', staged_snapshot_id = 'snapshot-1', snapshot_bundle_seq = 1, snapshot_row_count = 1 WHERE singleton_key = 1

cases.name: detach-mid-cleanup-failure-rolls-back
cases.description: A detach failure during cleanup rolls back local state; after restart and removing the failure, detach succeeds cleanly.

sql: CREATE TRIGGER fail_detach_attachment_update BEFORE UPDATE ON _sync_attachment_state BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'simulated detach crash'); END
